title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. ramosissimum
intro: Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. ramosissimum, also known as the variegated strawflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. ramosissimum include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its herbal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
